---
title: "Source Guide: Vectors and Linear Combinations"
description: "A curated source guide for learning vectors, span, and weighted combinations from first pass through research bridge."
page-type: source-guide
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/concepts/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose the right resource for first exposure, second pass, or paper bridge."
  - "Separate stable vector-space references from current representation-learning bridges."
  - "See which sources belong to the concept, application, and research pages in this package."
applications:
  - machine-learning
  - representation-learning
keywords:
  - source guide
  - vectors
  - span
  - embeddings
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "MIT 18.06SC Basis and Dimension"
  - "Stanford VMLS"
  - "Hefferon, Linear Algebra"
current-sources:
  - "Deep learning, transformers and graph neural networks: a linear algebra perspective (2025)"
  - "Attention Is All You Need"
---

## How To Use This Guide

Use one source per layer:

- `First pass` for intuition and basic fluency
- `Second pass` for more exercises, operator language, or implementation flavor
- `Paper bridge` for seeing vectors inside modern ML papers

Do not try to read the whole stack at once.

## Stable Core

- [MIT 18.06SC: Basis and Dimension](https://live.ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/resources/basis-and-dimension-1/) - `First pass` - strong official source for span, basis, and dimension as one storyline. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Introduction to Applied Linear Algebra – Vectors, Matrices, and Least Squares](https://web.stanford.edu/~boyd/vmls/) - `First pass` - very good applied framing for vectors, combinations, and matrix-based thinking. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - self-study depth with more proof and exercise practice. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Current Bridge

- [Deep learning, transformers and graph neural networks: a linear algebra perspective](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11075-025-02218-2) - `Second pass` - current bridge from vector-space basics to embeddings, attention, and graph models. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Paper Bridge

- [Attention is All you Need](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2017/hash/3f5ee243547dee91fbd053c1c4a845aa-Abstract.html) - `Paper bridge` - best first paper for seeing weighted vector mixtures become a central ML operation. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Path 1: First serious understanding

1. MIT 18.06SC Basis and Dimension
2. [Vectors and Linear Combinations](../concepts/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd)
3. Hefferon for extra exercises

### Path 2: Vectors to modern representations

1. [Vectors and Linear Combinations](../concepts/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd)
2. [Vector Mixtures in Embeddings and Attention](../applications/vector-mixtures-in-embeddings-and-attention.qmd)
3. [Attention as Weighted Vector Mixture](../paper-lab/attention-as-weighted-vector-mixture.qmd)

### Path 3: Build intuition visually

1. [Vectors and Linear Combinations](../concepts/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd)
2. [Computation Lab: Linear Combinations and Span Geometry](../labs/linear-combinations-and-span-geometry.qmd)
3. [Exercises: Vectors and Linear Combinations](../exercises/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd)

## Sources Checked Online

All links above were checked on `2026-04-24`.

The verification pass included:

- official MIT OCW pages
- official Stanford or author-maintained textbook pages
- official NeurIPS proceedings page
- current journal page for the 2025 linear-algebra perspective survey
